one_time_description: Crown Indigenous Relations and Northern Affairs Canada transfer of $15,000,000 (appears to be a one-time settlement or land claim payment; no prior year equivalent)
economic_notes: Nation has extensive GBE portfolio including forestry, marine terminals, liquor store, service station, resort, campsites, engineering, and fisheries partnerships. GBEs had combined revenue of $76,023,262 and net income of $14,195,504 in 2024. Subsequent event: acquired Bill 13 logging contract for $2,728,000 on June 24, 2024. 212 minors have trust funds totaling $775,530. Nation has a major building construction contract with $32,088,615 remaining costs expected to complete in fiscal 2026.
notes: Large and complex GBE portfolio with 21 entities accounted for under modified equity method. Three subsidiaries consolidated line-by-line: Quinsam Properties BT Inc., Quinsam Reserve Leasing Inc., and We Wai Kai Nation Healing Society. The $15,000,000 CIRNAC transfer appears one-time with no prior year equivalent. Income from GBEs jumped to $12,666,987 from $3,190,789 driven by major expansion in forestry and other partnerships. Long-term debt increased significantly from $9,377,134 to $31,981,972 due to a $26,605,300 FNFA demand loan secured against BC First Nations Gaming funds and forestry revenues. Total GBE assets of $223,375,678 are off-balance sheet. Community Development segment surplus of $11,719,663 and Economic Development surplus of $11,954,311 reflect large one-time transfers and GBE income. Remuneration schedule not included in provided text. Honoraria of $723,588 reported as expense object which may relate to Chief and Council. No population figures disclosed.
